English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Henry Phelps Gage papers
Gage, Henry Phelps.
1874
26 Linear Feet
1886-1955. Correspondence, diaries, notes, notebooks, slides, photogrpahs. Correspondence, diaries, notes on the life of Simon Henry Gage. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 36 37 42 - folder 1 ? (unprocessed) ======= 36 37 42 - folder 1 ? (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2088724
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Staff
Date completed:
May 1992August 2001
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, May 2004
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Henry Phelps Gage was a physicist and inventor, and son of Cornell University Professor Simon Henry Gage and Susanna Phelps Gage, also a scientist.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, diaries, notes on the life of Simon Henry Gage; notebooks on lectures at Ithaca High School and Cornell University; printed matter on physics and related subjects; slides, photos, photographic plates, and miscellaneous papers. Includes correspondence and other papers pertaining to Henry Phelps Gage's work as head of the Optical Laboratory of Corning Glass Works (1911-1947) and to his membership in various scientific societies; also personal and family correspondence, including papers of Susanna Phelps Gage.
Cite As:
Henry Phelps Gage papers, #1874. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Color slides. Notebooks. Diaries. Correspondence. Physics. Women scientists. Optics. Gage, Simon Henry, 1851-1944. Gage, Susanna Phelps. Ithaca High School, (Ithaca, N.Y.) Cornell University Corning Glass Works
